topic.name: Energy Policy and Renewable Resources
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing energy security, promoting renewable energy sources, and reducing carbon emissions through various incentives and regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 governing petroleum acquisition for the Strategic Petroleum Reserve
Allocates amounts from such Fund for solar energy technologies, ocean and wave energy, and carbon capture and storage.
Document 2: Requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles to comprise at least 50% qualified vehicles
Defines 'qualified vehicle' as a vehicle that operates solely on natural gas, hydrogen, or biodiesel.
Document 3: Allows seven-year amortization of mechanical or electronic systems that reduce carbon dioxide emissions
Allows a new 30% tax credit for investment in carbon dioxide capture, transport, and storage property.
Document 4: Allows a 30% tax credit for investment in a qualifying offshore wind facility
Requires the Secretary to review credits allocated under this Act.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code with respect to the tax credit for the production of electricity from advanced nuclear power facilities
Establishes requirements for the allocation of unutilized portions of the national megawatt capacity limitation.
Document 6: Amends the Petroleum Marketing Practices Act regarding renewable fuel pumps and tanks
Requires automobile manufacturers to ensure that at least 50% of 2015 and 2016 model year automobiles are dual fueled.
Document 7: Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the issuance of permits for emissions resulting from livestock production
Focuses on emissions associated with biological processes.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at improving water quality, conservation, and management in rural and coastal communities.

Document 1: authorizes the Department of the Interior to carry out projects for rural water systems
defines the service areas of such projects in North Dakota and Montana.
Document 2: Amends the Endangered Species Act to involve states in species protection
requires the Secretary to review the implementation and effectiveness of state protective actions.
Document 4: extends authorization for grants for coastal water quality monitoring
requires the Administrator to study benefits of molecular diagnostics for water quality.
Document 6: requires the EPA to establish a grant program to reduce lead levels in water
provides assistance to eligible entities for reducing lead in drinking water.
Document 7: deems requirements of the Endangered Species Act satisfied regarding water project operations
ensures water availability for other authorized project purposes.

topic.name: Medicare Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative amendments aimed at improving Medicare services, payment structures, and health incentives for beneficiaries.

Document 1: establish a minimum payment rate for primary care services
primary care physician
Document 2: incentives are provided to Medicare beneficiaries
annual wellness visit, tobacco cessation, diabetes screening test
Document 3: extend the current update to the single conversion factor
increase in the work geographic index
Document 4: repeal the annual limitation on deductibles under essential health benefits
employer-sponsored qualified health plans
Document 5: increase the number of diseases and conditions for which beneficiaries may be targeted for medication therapy management
comprehensive and targeted medication reviews
Document 6: exclude from the average sales price in calculating Medicare payments for drugs
customary prompt pay discounts
Document 7: repeals provisions of the Internal Revenue Code that impose fines on large employers
set limits on the annual deductible on health plans offered in the small group market

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Rights and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster comprises various legislative acts addressing rights related to life, employment, bankruptcy, patent laws, and redistricting reforms.

Document 1: Declares that the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human being beginning at the moment of fertilization.
Prohibits construing this Act to require the prosecution of any woman for the death of her unborn child.
Document 2: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives.
Requires such redistricting to be conducted according to a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state.
Document 3: Amends federal bankruptcy law with respect to the requirement for a municipality to be a debtor under Chapter 9.
Requires that, with respect to creditors who are the municipality's employees or retirees, 'good faith' has the same meaning as it does in the National Labor Relations Act.
Document 4: This bill amends the federal judicial code to allow patent actions to be brought only in judicial districts where the defendant has its principal place of business or is incorporated.
These provisions replace current patent action venue laws that allow patent actions to be brought in the judicial district where the defendant resides.
Document 5: Declares that no predispute arbitration agreement shall be valid or enforceable if it requires arbitration of an employment, consumer, or franchise dispute.
Exempts from this Act arbitration provisions in collective bargaining agreements.
Document 6: Amends federal patent law to revise the one-year grace period under the Leahy-Smith America Invents Act.
Requires the amendments made by this Act to take effect as if enacted as part of the AIA.
Document 7: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity by covered entities.

Provides for enforcement, including giving the EEOC, the Librarian of Congress, the Attorney General, and U.S. courts the same enforcement powers as they have under specified provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing services and support for vulnerable groups, including disabled individuals, infants, toddlers, Native Americans, and low-income families.

Document 1: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act to allow states to use special education grant funds to contract with entities that have the experience to assist disabled children.
Requires local educational agencies (LEAs) to provide a disabled child and his or her parents...
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to enable eligible states... to improve the quality of care for infants and toddlers.
Authorizes the use of grant funds to... support initiatives to improve the quality of the provider workforce.
Document 3: Reauthorize appropriations for a grant program to ensure the survival and continuing vitality of Native American languages.
Document 4: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992 to revise the purpose for grants for lead-based paint hazard reduction in target housing.
Makes eligible to apply for such a grant... an Indian tribe.
Document 5: Defines 'Indian general welfare benefit' as any payment made or services provided to or on behalf of a member of an Indian tribe under an Indian tribal government program.
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to... establish a Tribal Advisory Committee to advise the Secretary on the taxation of Indians.
Document 6: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services, in awarding grants for maternal, infant, and early childhood home visitation programs, to give priority to applications submitted by certain governmental entities.
A 'qualified community partner' is a local educational agency, elementary or secondary school...

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Framework
topic.description: This topic encompasses the establishment and modification of federal regulations, including the processes for rulemaking, judicial appointments, and oversight of energy and environmental policies.

Document 1: Establishes additional requirements for rulemaking under the Administrative Procedure Act (APA)
development of at not less than three regulatory options
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint four additional permanent district judges
Document 3: Amends the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974
Sets forth requirements for the President's transmittal to Congress
Document 4: authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to issue a temporary cease and desist order
grants FERC emergency authority to change or suspend temporarily the rates
Document 5: Requires the general notice of proposed rulemaking published in the Federal Register to include any scientific research
Defines 'scientific study' for purposes of the rulemaking process
Document 6: repeals requirements of the Mineral Leasing Act and the Energy Policy Act of 1992
limiting existing authority of the Forest Service to protect the national forests
Document 7: Amends the Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act of 2009 to repeal its sunset date
Establishes a private right of action to enforce compliance with such Act

topic.name: Regulatory Measures on Privacy and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing privacy protections and transparency in various sectors including broadcasting, law enforcement, and technology.

Document 1: prohibit the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from having the authority to require broadcasters to present opposing viewpoints on controversial issues
commonly referred to as the Fairness Doctrine.
Document 2: prohibit assistance under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program to a law enforcement agency that uses an automated license plate reader
ensure that data collected by the reader is not stored for a period exceeding 30 days.
Document 3: require states receiving funding under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program to use an incorporation system that requires applicants for forming corporations to provide and update lists of their beneficial owners
prescribes penalties for providing false or fraudulent beneficial ownership information.
Document 4: study and identify any potential threats to privacy protections posed by the integration of unmanned aircraft systems into the national airspace system
requires such procedures to ensure that the integration of drone systems into the national airspace system is done in compliance with privacy principles.
Document 5: require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to biennially inventory each radio spectrum band
make the information available to the public on a searchable Internet website.
Document 6: Makes it unlawful for a person during specified periods before a federal primary or general election to make certain prerecorded political telephone calls
disclosing immediately the recorded nature of the calls, the identity of the caller, and the caller's telephone number.
Document 7: prescribes federal administrative and licensing requirements governing Internet game-of-skill facilities
safeguards against fraud, money laundering, and terrorist finance.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating consumer financial products and protecting consumers in the lending and mortgage markets.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a creditor to make specified disclosures when making a payday loan
Prohibits a creditor from making a payday loan to any consumer unless specified notices are posted
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to establish a pilot program to encourage shared appreciation mortgage modifications
Requires a shared appreciation mortgage modification to reduce by specified action the loan-to-value ratio of a covered mortgage
Document 3: Abolishes the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and each Federal reserve bank
Document 4: Directs the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to require Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to disclose promptly the amount by which the purchase price was less than the minimum purchase price sufficient for approval
Document 5: Requires Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac to be considered an agency subject to the public information disclosure requirements of the Freedom of Information Act
Document 6: Prohibits a covered mortgagee from initiating a foreclosure unless the mortgagee has used its best efforts to determine whether the mortgagor is eligible for a qualified loan modification
Declares any sale of property in violation of this Act void
Document 7: Establishes in the Department of the Treasury the Office of National Insurance with regulatory oversight over national insurers
Authorizes the Commissioner to provide for the registration of insurance self-regulatory organizations and to supervise and regulate them

topic.name: Federal Legislation for Public Programs
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at establishing and supporting public programs in areas such as international volunteering, marine research, transportation, entitlement reform, smart manufacturing, and conservation efforts.

Document 1: establish and administer a Global Service Fellowship Program
promote international volunteering opportunities
Document 2: provides guidance and leadership to its state and territorial organizations
prohibits controlling or influencing specific activities
Document 3: extend the eligibility for projects of national and regional significance
a planned and coordinated multimodal freight and passenger transportation network
Document 4: establish the National Marine Mammal Research Program
undertaking research on marine mammals
Document 5: review Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid
make comprehensive recommendations to sustain the solvency and stability
Document 6: complete a national plan for smart manufacturing technology development
improve the productivity and energy efficiency of the U.S. manufacturing sector
Document 7: establishes the Salmon Stronghold Partnership
identify and conserve salmon strongholds

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to agricultural support, local food systems, and community food initiatives.

Document 1: Community-Supported Agriculture Promotion Act
promote community-supported agriculture
Document 4: Kosher and Halal Food Act of 2013
increase the purchase of Kosher and Halal food
Document 6: Local Farms, Food, and Jobs Act of 2011
inclusion of locally or regionally produced agricultural food products

topic.name: Veterans Affairs and Benefits
topic.description: Legislation and programs related to the support and benefits of veterans and military personnel.

Document 1: Authorizes payment under the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) beneficiary travel program of travel expenses in connection with medical examination, treatment, or care of a veteran with vision impairment, a spinal cord injury or disorder, or double or multiple amputations.
travel is in connection with inpatient care in a VA special disabilities rehabilitation program.
Document 2: Military Personnel Toxic Exposure Registry Act - Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to establish and administer a system to identify members of the Armed Forces who were potentially exposed to a hazardous disposal site.
Requires the Secretary to... report annually to the congressional defense committees on the status of system implementation and incidences of illnesses which may have been caused by such exposure.
Document 3: Allows an individual to elect to receive retired pay for non-regular (reserve) service upon retirement for service performed in an active reserve status after attaining eligibility for regular retirement.

Requires the recomputation of reserve retired pay and, if appropriate, the adjustment of the retired grade of reserve retirees to reflect the successful completion of at least two years of post-retirement service in an active reserve status.
Document 4: Recognizes the tireless work and dedication of the Missing in America Project, in conjunction with numerous veterans service organizations, in identifying unclaimed remains of veterans.

Requires the Secretary, upon a positive determination with respect to any such remains, to provide for and cover the cost of the burial and funeral expenses if the Secretary determines that... there are no other sufficient resources to cover such expenses.
Document 6: Veterans Pensions Protection Act - Excludes monetary veterans' benefits paid to a veteran from a state or municipality from consideration as income for purposes of pension benefits paid by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA).
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a Patriot Express Loan Program to guarantee loans made by express lenders to eligible members of the military community.
Includes as eligible members: (1) a veteran, including a service-disabled veteran; (2) a member of the Armed Forces on active duty who is eligible to participate in the Transition Assistance Program.

topic.name: Mental Health and Healthcare Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving mental health services and healthcare access for various populations, particularly in educational and community settings.

Document 1: Mental Health First Aid training program
recognize the signs and symptoms of mental illness
Document 2: training in home health care to nurses
grants to visiting nurse associations
Document 3: loan repayment program for primary care physicians
community health centers with a shortage of primary care physicians
Document 4: grants to operate community health centers
services to medically underserved populations
Document 5: minimum standards for residential programs
serving children with emotional, behavioral, or mental health problems
Document 6: plan for the prevention, control, and management of hepatitis C virus
increase awareness of HCV
Document 7: prevent suicides among all ages
enhance services for students with mental health or substance use disorders at institutions of higher education

topic.name: Education Policy and Access
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving access to education and supporting various educational programs for students, particularly those from disadvantaged backgrounds.

Document 1: prohibit the provision of federal education funding to state or local educational agencies
provide students with health services
Document 2: allow state leadership activities grants to be used to provide technical assistance
study the effectiveness of distance learning or self-study programs
Document 3: award competitive five-year grants to certain nonprofit organizations
encourage disadvantaged students to attend institutions of higher education
Document 4: increase the maximum Pell Grant award
to increase access to higher education
Document 5: allow a state to withdraw from the Common Core State Standards
academic standards common to a significant number of states
Document 6: establish a Connecting Education and Emerging Professions Demonstration Grant program
develop school- and work-based curricula or programs
Document 7: integrate financial literacy education into public elementary and secondary schools
train secondary school financial literacy or entrepreneurship teachers

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and recreational use of federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the state of Alaska King Cove Road and Sitkinak Island
Requires the King Cove Road conveyance to be used for construction of a state road.
Document 2: Requires federal public land management officials to facilitate the use of, and access to, federal public lands for fishing, sport hunting, and recreational shooting
Requires Bureau of Land Management (BLM) and Forest Service lands to be open to recreational fishing, hunting, and shooting.
Document 3: Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the importation of polar bear parts taken legally in sport hunts in Canada.
Document 4: Bars the Secretary of the Interior from approving a management plan for a National Heritage Area unless the local coordinating entity provides written notification
Prohibits any employee of the National Park Service from entering private property without the owner's written consent.
Document 5: Authorizes Sealaska Corporation to select and receive conveyance of its remaining land entitlement under the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete the conveyance of lands to Sealaska within one year of selection.
Document 6: Amends the Washington State Wilderness Act of 1984 to specifically allow the operation and maintenance of Green Mountain Lookout
Prohibits the Chief of the Forest Service from moving the Lookout from its current location unless necessary.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and programs aimed at supporting and enhancing small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act relating to the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program
require certain program directives issued for the general conduct of SBIR programs
Document 2: Amends provisions of the Small Business Act relating to the Express Loan Program
increase from 50% to 75% the maximum percentage of Program loans that may be guaranteed by the Small Business Administration (SBA)
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to reduce state matching funds requirements
Revises the SBA's Rural Outreach program to increase states' participation in the SBIR and STTR programs
Document 4: Requires the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and carry out a program for providing education and resources to small business concerns
assists them in understanding the dangers and identification of phishing
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to guarantee the payment of up to $4 billion per fiscal year
encourage the formation and growth of small businesses
Document 6: Extends through July 31, 2009, under the same terms and conditions, the authorization for any program, authority, or provision
currently authorized through March 20, 2009, under the Small Business Act or the Small Business Investment Act of 1958
Document 7: Requires the Director of the Minority Business Development Agency to establish the Minority Business Development Program
provide qualified minority businesses with technical assistance, loan guarantees, and contract procurement assistance

topic.name: Immigration and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at regulating immigration, enhancing national security, and addressing the implications of terrorism on immigration policies.

Document 1: Passport Fee Relief Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a tax credit for the cost of obtaining travel documents necessary to comply with the terrorist travel and effective screening plan.
necessary to comply with the terrorist travel and effective screening plan developed under the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS), acting through the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), to submit to Congress a plan for ensuring the placement of sufficient CBP officers at each of the 10 U.S. international airports with the largest volume of international travelers.
Document 3: Dairy and Sheep H-2A Act or the DASH Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category an alien coming temporarily to the United States to work as a sheepherder, goat herder, or dairy worker.
provides for a three-year initial period of admission with additional three-year extensions.
Document 4: Terrorist Denaturalization and Passport Revocation Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include among the grounds for loss of U.S. nationality by a native-born or naturalized citizen: taking an oath or making a declaration of allegiance to a foreign terrorist organization after attaining the age of 18.

Amends the Passport Act of 1926 to prohibit... the Secretary of State from issuing a passport or passport card to an individual who is a member of a foreign terrorist organization engaged in hostilities against the United States or its allies.
Document 5: Families First Immigration Enforcement Act - Requires the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), in a workplace enforcement operation that is calculated to apprehend or does apprehend at least 50 aliens, to give state agencies sufficient notice.
States that detainees should be held within the jurisdiction of the local United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) field office to the extent that space allows.
Document 6: Security and Fairness Enhancement for America Act of 2015 or the SAFE for America Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the diversity immigrant program.
Document 7: Emergency Immigration Workload Reduction and Homeland Security Enhancement Act of 2007 - Suspends the following immigration-related authorities: the visa waiver program; adjustment to permanent resident status; renewal of temporary protected status (TPS); the diversity immigrant visa program.
Requires U.S. visa issuance to be discontinued in a country which has 24 or more listed individuals.

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing crime victim rights, combating exploitation, and improving law enforcement practices.

Document 1: Securing Adolescents From Exploitation-Online Act of 2007 or the SAFE Act of 2007
expand the reporting requirements of electronic communication and remote computing service providers with respect to violations of child sexual exploitation and pornography laws.
Document 2: Justice for Crime Victims Act of 2014
the right to be informed in a timely manner of any plea bargain or deferred prosecution agreement.
Document 3: Maritime Law Enforcement Improvement Act of 2007
Makes it unlawful for a person to transport, harbor, or conceal an alien on board a vessel.
Document 4: Secure Access to Firearms Enhancement (SAFE) Act of 2011
provides for reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states.
Document 5: No More Tulias: Drug Law Enforcement Evidentiary Standards Improvement Act of 2009
Prohibits a state from receiving for a fiscal year any drug control and system improvement grant funds.
Document 6: Denying Firearms and Explosives to Dangerous Terrorists Act of 2007
grants the Attorney General the authority to deny the transfer of firearms or the issuance of a federal firearms and explosives license.
Document 7: Former Vice President Protection Act of 2008
provides secret service protection to former Vice Presidents, their spouses, and their children under 16 years of age.

topic.name: Federal Budget and Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to federal budget management, appropriations, and funding for various programs and services.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to issue a benefit guarantee certificate to each individual entitled to benefits under title II of the Social Security Act.
represents the obligation of the federal government to provide for payment of OASDI benefits.
Document 2: authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs to carry out a pilot program under which it may accept donations from specified non-federal entities.
the VA may accept such a donation only if the property is identified as meeting a VA need.
Document 3: Makes specified provisional continuing appropriations in the event that any regular appropriation bill for a fiscal year is not enacted.
prevents a federal government shutdown.
Document 4: Appropriates $200 billion for quarterly payments to the states by the Secretary of the Treasury.
Prohibits the use of such funds to supplement state capital budgets.
Document 5: Expands the urbanized area formula grants program to include public transit projects.
Establishes certain grant use limits for the operating costs of public transportation equipment.
Document 6: Revises requirements for the equity bonus program for specified federal-aid highway programs.
Increases from 92% to 105% the percentage for such a state of the total fiscal year apportionments.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Transportation to withhold 15% of a state's apportionment of certain federal-aid highway funds.
Requires funds withheld from a state to be deposited in the Treasury and used for deficit.

topic.name: Government Transparency and Access
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving public access to government information and services.

Document 1: requires an executive agency to provide notice and an opportunity for public comment before relocating any agency office
develop a plan to address the impact
Document 2: establishes an independent Commission to Improve Labor Statistics
formulate recommendations for any improvement to such process
Document 3: requires the heads of agencies to ensure that their respective chief information officers have a significant role in information technology decisions
prohibits agencies from contracting for IT without their CIO's review and approval
Document 4: amends the Freedom of Information Act concerning requests involving access to records relating to federal contracts
requires an agency to release requested information unless shown to be exempt
Document 5: establishes the Commission on Freedom of Information Act Processing Delays to conduct a study
make recommendations on how the use of exemptions may be limited
Document 6: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act with respect to civil liability for noncompliance
requires a consumer reporting agency to provide free disclosures
Document 7: requires the Public Printer to establish and maintain a website accessible by the public
requires each federal agency to provide the Public Printer with an electronic copy of congressionally mandated reports

topic.name: Military Funding and Operations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the funding, construction, and operational support of military and veteran affairs.

Document 1: Appropriates funds for FY2008 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force
Appropriates funds for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for various veterans programs.
Document 2: Prohibits funding the transfer of personnel or functions from Fort Monmouth until an audit is completed
Ensures support to the Global War on Terror or other critical operations is not disrupted.
Document 3: Amends the Detainee Treatment Act to require procedures for the interrogation of unprivileged enemy belligerents
Requires the Secretary to notify Congress about interrogation modifications.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of each military department to assign Sexual Assault Nurse Examiners to each brigade
Requires appropriate Examiner training and certification.
Document 5: Requires the President to complete the redeployment of National Guard units serving in Operation Iraqi Freedom
Mandates the return of essential National Guard equipment used in such Operation.
Document 6: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for procurement
Limits total procurement costs to $3.1 billion.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense to transfer at least 10% of eligible equipment to federal and state agencies
Defines eligible equipment for law enforcement activities.

topic.name: U.S. Trade Legislation and Diplomacy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to trade agreements, tariff suspensions, and diplomatic initiatives with various countries.

Document 1: Amends the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act to make permanent the extension of temporary duty-free treatment of certain textile and apparel articles manufactured in a beneficiary country (particularly, Costa Rica) and imported into the United States.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic staple fibers.
Document 3: United States-China Diplomatic Expansion Act of 2007 - Authorizes FY2008 appropriations for public diplomacy programming, hiring of additional local public diplomacy personnel, and public diplomacy information technology infrastructure in the People's Republic of China.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on asulam sodium salt.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on aircraft grade polyvinyl butyral.
Document 7: Amends the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act, as amended by the Haitian Hemispheric Opportunity through Partnership Encouragement Act of 2006, to extend the date by which the President must determine whether Haiti is meeting specified political, economic, social, and security requirements in order for Haiti to qualify for preferential trade provisions under such Act.

topic.name: Child Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting children from hazardous substances and ensuring safety standards in consumer products.

Document 1: requires any nicotine provided in a liquid nicotine container to be packaged in accordance with CPSC's standards
standard for the special packaging of a household substance under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act
Document 4: bans children's products containing more than specified amounts of lead
defines children's product to mean any consumer product marketed for use by children under age six
Document 7: amends the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act to revise the limit on lead content in children's products
prohibits the CPSC from requiring third party testing of children's products until specified conditions are met

topic.name: Oil and Gas Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to the exploration, production, and regulation of oil and gas resources in the United States.

Document 1: Revises federal pipeline safety requirements to authorize states to enforce state requirements for the safe construction and operation of underground gas storage wellbores.
FERC fails to approve such requirement within 30 days after its submission.
Document 2: Prohibit the Department of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas in any area of the outer Continental Shelf off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.
Document 3: Directs the President to limit the application of law as necessary to expedite the exploration and production of oil and gas from oil shale and tar sands.
Grants U.S. district courts exclusive jurisdiction to hear all causes or claims arising from any action undertaken, or any decision made, by the President pursuant to this Act.
Document 4: Repeals provisions of the Energy Policy Act of 2005 relating to incentives for production from marginal oil wells.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to suspend royalty relief for producers of oil or natural gas on federal lands during periods in which oil and natural gas production is at certain levels.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement a competitive leasing program for the exploration, development, and production of the oil and gas resources on the Coastal Plain of Alaska.

Amends the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act of 1980 to repeal the prohibition against leasing or other development leading to production of oil and gas from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 6: Extends provisions prohibiting the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency from requiring an NPDES permit for a covered vessel for any discharge.
Covers effluent from properly functioning marine engines and other incidental discharges.
Document 7: Expresses the sense of Congress that the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources to the United States.
Amends the Naval Petroleum Reserves Production Act of 1976 to require the mandatory program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the NPR.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the affordability and management of student loans for borrowers, particularly those in military service or facing financial hardship.

Document 1: phase-in cuts in the interest rate charged undergraduate student borrowers
reducing such rate from 6.8% in July 2006 to 3.4% in July 2011
Document 2: require the Secretary of Education to verify eligibility for income contingent repayment
eligible to participate in the public service loan forgiveness program
Document 4: extend the 6% interest rate limitation on debt entered into during a servicemember's military service
to consolidate or refinance one or more student loans incurred before such service
Document 5: establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal, accrued unpaid interest, and late charges
eligibility requirements based on a borrower's income or debt-to-income ratio

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at adjusting tax liabilities, providing exemptions, and reforming tax policies.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%
Expresses the sense of the Senate that Congress should enact tax reform that repeals unfair and unnecessary tax loopholes
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt fixed-wing aircraft used to provide acute care emergency medical services from certain restrictions on tax-exempt bond financing.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to permit a state to offset state income tax obligations owed by nonresidents against any federal income tax refunds due to such nonresidents
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to treat annuity payments to a surviving child of an eligible participant under the military's Survivor Benefit Plan as the earned income of such child
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to limit the penalty for failure to disclose a reportable transaction
Expands the continuous tax levy on payments to vendors for goods and services to include payments for all property, goods, or services.
Document 6: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2017, the income tax credit for biodiesel and renewable diesel used as fuel.
Document 7: Repeals provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 eliminating the tax on estates and generation-skipping transfers
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for an increase to $3.5 million of the estate tax exclusion.
